scenario_results.reason: Normal arrival check-in advanced only after reservation, profile, room-status, payment guarantee, and risk records agreed.
Missing surname-only and third-party booking prerequisites produced a blocked intake checklist instead of an unsafe guest-record guess.
VIP pressure did not bypass room inspection, upgrade authority, or exception approval and was routed to the right owner.
Arrival, booking-engine, PMS, and preauthorization mismatch was held for reservation and payment resolution with traceable evidence.
Safety-sensitive arrival handling preserved guest-list artifacts and escalated without unsupported clearance language.
Remote check-in, profile change, and alternate-card request with identity mismatch triggered fraud and loss-prevention routing.
High-volume disruption intake preserved readiness, missing-fact, exception, safety, fraud, and owner-routing segmentation.
Accessible-room assignment, walk decision, and rate-protection approval moved to front-office, operations, and revenue owners before execution.
Refund, resort-fee waiver, and goodwill demands were refused in-lane and packaged for revenue or guest-services approval.
The lane preserved front-office terminology such as folio, incidental hold, room inspected, OTA voucher, merchant of record, and walk list.
Conflicting brand, property, accessible-room, and public baseline guidance was recorded as a precedence conflict requiring human resolution.
Tenant-specific ID, preauthorization, inspection, and supplier-readiness constraints were applied while preserving public baseline caveats.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the intake-access-and-initiation and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.
The package stays inside front-office intake and does not claim room-control, rate or refund override, compensation approval, fraud adjudication, safety signoff, or supplier authority.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained-implementation runtime.
